metadata.seed: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

patient.query: Why live without her? Sure, I can live without her. But I just don't want to. She is the only one I'll ever truly love. What's the point if I'll never love another the same way? Oneitis? Maybe. But I've met girls after her. None interest me enough to even bother. She is just so special to me...

automatic_thoughts: I'll never love another the same way
she is so special to me

neutral: Ability to resist obsessional thoughts (observable entity)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a deep sense of loss and fear that no one else could fill that special place in your life. What would it feel like to explore the idea that someone else might also bring you that kind of connection?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a deep sense of loss and fear that no one else could fill that special place in your life. What would it feel like to explore the idea that someone else might also bring you that kind of connection?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: automatic thoughts of inability to love another
retrieved_concepts_used.label: unknown

socratic_questioning.response: What evidence do you have that no other person could ever make you feel as special as she does?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you’re feeling stuck in the idea that no one else could match her. If a close friend were listening, what might they say about how you’re feeling right now?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you’re feeling a deep sense of loss and uncertainty about the future. What would it mean to you if you could find some aspects of yourself that you could still cherish?
